list.title: Securing the AI Agent: A Unified Framework for Multi-Layer Agent Red Teaming

extra.desc: AI-Infra-Guard is an open-source framework that addresses AI infrastructure security through layered detection paradigms spanning infrastructure, protocol, agent behavior, and model layers.

list.title: AGE: Adaptive-masking for Graph Embedding in Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation

extra.desc: GraphRAG extends RAG by incorporating graph-structured data for LLMs, addressing latent feature misalignment through Adaptive-masking for Graph Embedding (AGE) that uses Transformer-based self-supervised learning with learnable node sampling.

list.title: The Mirage of Optimizing Training Policies: Monotonic Inference Policies as the Real Objective for LLM Reinforcement Learning

extra.desc: Training-inference mismatch in reinforcement learning for large language models leads to instability, which is addressed through a new policy optimization objective and framework that ensures consistent policy improvements between training and inference phases.

list.title: Embodied.cpp: A Portable Inference Runtime of Embodied AI Models on Heterogeneous Robots

extra.desc: Embodied.cpp is a portable C++ runtime that enables efficient deployment of vision-language-action and world-action models across heterogeneous edge devices through modular execution layers and optimized inference.

list.title: Scaling Laws for Grid-Based Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search in High Dimensions

extra.desc: Grid-based multiprobe algorithms demonstrate superior dimensional scaling properties compared to graph-, tree-, and partitioning-based methods for approximate nearest neighbor search, making them competitive for high-dimensional and rebuild-heavy applications.

list.title: Parameter-Efficient Quantum-Inspired Fast Weight Programmers for Traffic-Matrix Forecasting

extra.desc: Quantum-inspired recurrent models using gated QKAN-FWPs demonstrate superior forecasting accuracy with reduced computational requirements compared to traditional LSTM networks for traffic matrix prediction.
aiMatchedKeywords: gated quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold network fast-weight programmers

list.title: WARP: Weight-Space Analysis for Recovering Training Data Portfolios

extra.desc: WARP is a framework that infers training data compositions from released model weights by analyzing geometric footprints in weight space through model merging and feature extraction.

list.title: AutoMem: Automated Learning of Memory as a Cognitive Skill

extra.desc: Memory management in large language models is treated as a trainable skill through a framework that automates both memory structure optimization and proficiency enhancement, leading to significant performance improvements in long-horizon tasks.

list.title: DuoMem: Towards Capable On-Device Memory Agents via Dual-Space Distillation

extra.desc: DuoMem is a dual-space distillation framework that transfers procedural problem-solving from large language models to compact student models through context-space and parameter-space distillation, achieving high performance with minimal additional parameters and improved inference speed.

list.title: Logit-Contribution Scoring Identifies Non-Literal Retrieval Heads

extra.desc: Logit-Contribution Scoring (LOCOS) identifies attention heads responsible for non-literal context synthesis in large language models by measuring their output-value circuit's contribution to answer tokens, outperforming existing methods on retrieval benchmarks.

list.title: AGVBench: A Reliability-Oriented Benchmark of Data Augmentation for Vein Recognition

extra.desc: Research evaluates 30 augmentation strategies for vein recognition across multiple datasets and architectures, revealing inconsistencies between accuracy and adversarial security while highlighting dataset-specific effectiveness variations.

list.title: Transferability for General Reasoning: An Automated Curriculum for Multi-Domain RLVR

extra.desc: Transfer-Aware Curriculum (TAC) improves multi-domain reinforcement learning by prioritizing domains that provide broad benefits to other domains, using gradient-geometry alignment to estimate cross-domain transferability.

list.title: InstanceControl: Controllable Complex Image Generation without Instance Labeling

extra.desc: InstanceControl enables multi-instance image generation by using vision-language models to establish instance-level correspondences between text prompts and visual conditions, while employing adaptive mask refinement for improved accuracy.

list.title: Breaking Failure Cascades: Step-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Medical Multimodal Reasoning

extra.desc: A reinforcement learning approach called MRPO is introduced to improve clinical image reasoning by addressing cascading errors through step-wise process rewards, demonstrating superior performance over existing methods.

list.title: EvoPolicyGym: Evaluating Autonomous Policy Evolution in Interactive Environments

extra.desc: Autonomous agents evaluate policy improvement through iterative editing within fixed budgets, revealing that successful policy evolution requires both task-specific mechanisms and feedback-constrained refinement.

list.title: AgenticSTS: A Bounded-Memory Testbed for Long-Horizon LLM Agents

extra.desc: A bounded contract approach for long-horizon LLM agents uses typed retrieval to assemble fresh prompts, enabling isolated analysis of memory components and demonstrating improved performance in complex decision-making tasks.

list.title: From SRA to Self-Flow: Data Augmentation or Self-Supervision?

extra.desc: Research investigates the mechanisms behind self-alignment methods in diffusion transformers, finding that performance improvements stem primarily from data augmentation along the noise dimension rather than token interactions between noise levels.

list.title: Discrete Diffusion Language Models for Interactive Radiology Report Drafting

extra.desc: Diffusion language models match or exceed autoregressive models in medical visual question answering while offering faster decoding and bidirectional text editing capabilities.

list.title: When Search Agents Should Ask: DiscoBench for Clarification-Aware Deep Search

extra.desc: DiscoBench evaluates search agents' ability to handle ambiguous queries through clarification questioning and recovery in multi-step information-seeking tasks across diverse real-world domains.

list.title: SkillCoach: Self-Evolving Rubrics for Evaluating and Enhancing Agentic Skill-Use

extra.desc: SkillCoach is a self-evolving rubric framework that evaluates and improves agentic skill-use by analyzing skill selection, following, composition, and reflection processes, providing better supervision than outcome-only metrics.

list.title: PACE: A Proxy for Agentic Capability Evaluation

extra.desc: PACE is a framework that predicts expensive agentic LLM benchmark performance using a small subset of atomic evaluation instances, achieving high accuracy at a fraction of the cost.

list.title: Representation Distribution Matching for One-Step Visual Generation

extra.desc: Representation Distribution Matching enables high-quality image generation by matching feature distributions under pretrained encoders, with improved performance through optimized batch sizes and multi-encoder evaluation metrics.

list.title: Morphing into Hybrid Attention Models

extra.desc: FlashMorph is an efficient layer selection method that formulates hybrid layer selection as a budget-constrained optimization problem, using morphable models and linearization regularization to improve long-context efficiency in Transformers.

list.title: Learning to Move Before Learning to Do: Task-Agnostic pretraining for VLAs

list.title: Multi-Resolution Flow Matching: Training-Free Diffusion Acceleration via Staged Sampling

extra.desc: MrFlow accelerates text-to-image diffusion by combining low-resolution generation with pixel-space super-resolution and noise injection, achieving up to 25x speedup without training or runtime modifications.

list.title: Denser neq Better: Limits of On-Policy Self-Distillation for Continual Post-Training

list.title: AnyGroundBench: A Specialized-Domain Benchmark for Video Grounding in Vision-Language Models

list.title: Program-as-Weights: A Programming Paradigm for Fuzzy Functions
